bullets.text: Russia has threatened more strikes on Kyiv and advised foreign nationals to leave after the Ukrainian capital experienced one of the largest aerial assaults of the war overnight.
Israel's Prime Minister Netanyahu announced that Israel would intensify strikes against Hezbollah, with the Israeli military already targeting eastern Lebanon.
The World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo is 'outpacing' response efforts, urging neighboring countries to take immediate action.

Leaked internal documents from BHP, provided to The Guardian and ABC's Four Corners, indicate a significant backtrack on the company's climate change commitments, despite previously labeling it an 'existential' threat.
The Albanese government is moving forward with its plans for Capital Gains Tax (CGT) and negative gearing reforms, while also considering potential carve-outs for businesses.
A second group of ISIS-linked women and children are scheduled to arrive in Australia today, with some individuals potentially facing terrorism charges.
Emerging market assets, including stocks and currencies, saw a boost as prospects of a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz led to a decline in oil prices and increased investor risk appetite.
Gold prices advanced as the potential US-Iran deal, which could restore oil flows, helped temper inflation concerns among investors.
Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar announced that a political accord with the European Union's executive on the release of frozen funds is expected to be signed on May 28.
